# UC-22.51.19 · Safety Instrumented System (SIS) Cybersecurity Protection

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Safety, Industrial-Protocol &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*The safety system is what stops the plant if something dangerous happens. We watch every switch flip on it, every bypass, every test — and ring the bell immediately if anyone touches it without the right safety paperwork or in the wrong way.*

---

## Description

Tracks every SIS event — programme-mode entry, bypass-switch state, partial-stroke-test status — reconciles against the MOC and authorised-programmer lookups, and surfaces unauthorised activity or missing safety-test evidence.

## Value

SIS is the last line of safety; an attacker who can suppress an SIS trip or bypass a safety loop has the potential for catastrophic process outcomes (Triton/TRISIS). NCA OTCC inspectors will pull SIS evidence with the highest scrutiny. This UC produces continuous evidence of SIS protection at the same depth used for general OT — with stricter thresholds and faster escalation.

## Implementation

(1) Onboard SIS vendor diagnostic feeds via vendor-supplied HEC or syslog forwarders. (2) Onboard physical bypass-switch alarms via PLC contact-state events into `index=sis`. (3) Maintain `sis_inventory.csv` separately from the general OT inventory. (4) Maintain `sis_authorised_programmers.csv` — a deliberately small list (typically <10 named people per SIS instance, all functional-safety certified). (5) Maintain `pst_schedule.csv` with last partial-stroke-test date and interval per SIS valve. (6) Schedule UC-22.51.19 hourly. P1 notable on any flag.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.51.19` archived hourly to `audit_evidence` with `otcc_clause="OTCC-2-15-1-1"`. Each row preserves (sis_asset_key, asset_class, event_class, action, src_ip, src_user, change_summary, bypass_switch_state, partial_stroke_test_status, programme_mode_active, observed_at, unauthorised_programme_mode_flag, unauthorised_bypass_flag, missing_pst_evidence_flag). Dashboard `NCA OTCC – SIS Cybersecurity` shows live SIS health, bypass-status, programme-mode timeline, and 13-month trend.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Place a test SIS logic solver into programme mode without a paired MOC ticket. Within 30 minutes UC-22.51.19 MUST surface the event with `unauthorised_programme_mode_flag=true`, route a P1 notable to OT SOC and process-safety engineer, and archive the row.

### Negative scenario

Programme mode entered for a documented proof-test maintenance window with an MOC ticket and functional-safety review MUST NOT be flagged.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- HEC token dedicated to SIS-vendor feeds, with strict token scope (only writes to `index=sis`).
- SIS vendor onboarding (the four most common in process industries):
  - Schneider Triconex via Tristation / TriStationLink: vendor publishes diagnostic events to a Windows event service; Splunk UF on the Tristation server forwards to `index=sis` with `sourcetype=schneider:triconex:diag`.
  - Honeywell Safety Manager (SM) via the Safety Manager event log: UF on the SM Server forwards.
  - Siemens SIMATIC Safety (S7-1500F / Distributed Safety) via OPC UA + custom scripted input.
  - ABB SafetyGuard via DBC against the configuration database.
  - Emerson DeltaV SIS via syslog + DeltaV Diagnostics interface.
- Bypass-switch monitoring: physical bypass switches must route a contact-closure to a PLC input that publishes a heartbeat to `sis:bypass:alarm` via HEC every 60 seconds. The cabling and PLC logic for this is a design item in the SIS architecture; if missing, this UC's bypass detection is blind.
- Cisco Cyber Vision sensors on the SIS network bus for protocol-level detection.
- MOC ticketing in ServiceNow MOC module or equivalent, with explicit `sis_asset_key` field on each ticket.
- `index=sis` (84-month / 7-year retention), `index=cybervision`, `index=moc`, `index=audit_evidence`.
- Lookups:
  - `sis_inventory.csv` — owned by Process Safety Engineering. Columns: `sis_asset_key`, `asset_class`, `sis_function`, `tag_protection_level`, `safety_function`, `sif_id`, `sil_class`, `installed_date`, `proof_test_interval_months`.
  - `sis_authorised_programmers.csv` — owned by Process Safety Engineering plus Head of OT Security. Columns: `src_user`, `programmer_authorised_flag`, `fs_certification`, `cert_expires_at`. Refreshed monthly.
  - `pst_schedule.csv` — owned by Process Safety Engineering. Columns: `sis_asset_key`, `last_pst_at`, `pst_interval_days`, `next_pst_due_at`, `pst_owner_role`.
  - KV Store `otcc_22_51_19_exceptions`.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Each SIS vendor stanza below illustrates the pattern; the specific identifiers depend on the vendor's documentation.

Tristation server (Schneider Triconex) via UF on Tristation server:

```ini
[WinEventLog://Application]
disabled = false
whitelist = TristationLink
index = sis
sourcetype = schneider:triconex:diag
renderXml = true
```

HEC bypass-switch alarms: each plant has 50–500 bypass switches; PLC logic publishes a JSON event every 60 seconds (heartbeat) plus a discrete event on state-change:

```json
{"sis_asset_key": "SIS-VLV-A1-001", "event_class": "bypass_state", "action": "state_report", "bypass_switch_state": "normal", "observed_at": "2026-05-13T09:00:00Z"}
{"sis_asset_key": "SIS-VLV-A1-001", "event_class": "bypass_state", "action": "bypass_engaged", "bypass_switch_state": "bypassed", "observed_at": "2026-05-13T09:14:23Z"}
```

Validate the SIS feed:

```spl
index=sis earliest=-1d
| stats count by sourcetype event_class
```

Programme-mode entries, bypass state-changes, and PST completions should all be non-zero on a healthy estate.

## Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Four flags:

- `unauthorised_programme_mode_flag` if programme mode entered without MOC or by an unauthorised programmer.
- `unauthorised_bypass_flag` if a bypass engaged without MOC.
- `missing_pst_evidence_flag` if PST overdue per the schedule.
- Unexpected trip events (`event_class=trip_diagnostic AND action=unexpected_trip`) also surface as evidence of potential SIS health issues.

All four are P1 events given the SIS criticality.

**Schedule**:

```ini
[UC-22.51.19: Safety Instrumented System (SIS) Cybersecurity Protection]
cron_schedule = 35 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -2h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable, email
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.51.19,reg=NCA_OTCC,clause=OTCC-2-15-1-1
action.notable.param.rule_title = NCA OTCC SIS cybersecurity anomaly
action.notable.param.security_domain = endpoint
action.notable.param.severity = critical
action.email.to = ot-soc@example.com,process-safety-on-call@example.com,head-of-ot-security@example.com
alert.severity = 5
```

Notable creation on any flag. Always page Process Safety on-call given the criticality.

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Feed health**:

```spl
index=sis earliest=-1h | stats count by sourcetype
```

Missing sourcetypes indicate a broken pipeline; SIS feeds are P1 dependencies.

**(b) Inventory health**:

```spl
| inputlookup sis_inventory.csv
| stats count
```

Reconcile annually against the SIL-assessment register.

**(c) Authorised-programmer freshness**:

```spl
| inputlookup sis_authorised_programmers.csv
| eval expired=if(strptime(cert_expires_at, "%Y-%m-%d") < now(), "yes", "no")
| stats count by expired
```

No expired entries should exist.

**(d) PST schedule coverage**:

```spl
| inputlookup pst_schedule.csv
| eval days_since=round((now()-strptime(last_pst_at,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"))/86400)
| eval overdue=if(days_since>pst_interval_days,"yes","no")
| stats count by overdue
```

**(e) Positive control**: per `controlTest.positiveScenario`. Confirm P1 notable.

**(f) Negative control**: per `controlTest.negativeScenario`.

## Step 4 — Operationalize

**Dashboard**: `NCA OTCC – SIS Cybersecurity`.

- **Row 1, single values**: 'Programme-mode entries now', 'Bypass engaged', 'PSTs overdue', 'Unexpected trips 7d'.
- **Row 2, timeline**: programme-mode entry/exit per SIS asset, 30 days.
- **Row 3, table**: every flagged event with drill-through to the MOC ticket and the functional-safety review document.
- **Row 4, 13-month trend**: per SIS asset.
- **Row 5, valve PST coverage**: heatmap of weeks-since-PST vs interval per valve.

**Runbook for `unauthorised_programme_mode_flag=true`** (owner: OT SOC, escalating immediately to Process Safety on-call and Head of OT Security):

1. **Within 5 minutes**: page the process-safety on-call engineer. Pull the historian's process state for the affected unit.
2. **Within 15 minutes**: confirm whether the entry is operationally legitimate (forgotten MOC) or potentially malicious. If unclear, treat as malicious until proven otherwise; isolate the SIS logic solver from the network.
3. **Containment**: do not disconnect SIS from the process — SIS continues protecting the plant even when isolated from the engineering workstation. The risk path is the engineering workstation, not the SIS itself.
4. **Forensic preservation**: capture SIS configuration snapshot via the vendor's diagnostic export. Capture historian process variables for 1 hour before and 4 hours after.
5. **Escalate**: P1 OT cybersecurity incident under UC-22.51.16. Considered a safety-classification incident, requiring NCA reporting under UC-22.51.17.

**Runbook for `unauthorised_bypass_flag=true`**:

1. **Immediate**: process-safety on-call confirms whether bypass is needed for the current operation or is an error.
2. If error: instruct operator to remove bypass immediately. Document.
3. If genuine maintenance: raise the MOC retrospectively per safety-MOC protocols, plus a P3 governance ticket for the missed pre-event MOC.
4. Track bypass state at 5-minute granularity for the duration via a dedicated KV Store `sis_bypass_state` so operators can see exactly how long the SIS was bypassed for.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting

**SIS vendor feed silence** — the most common cause is the Tristation / SM Server stopping or its UF being uninstalled (sometimes by well-intentioned IT during patching). Cross-reference UC-22.51.13 logging-health which should catch the silence within 6 hours; for SIS, fix within 1 hour.

**Bypass-switch heartbeat absent** — the PLC logic publishing the heartbeat may have been edited out. Cross-reference UC-22.51.6 baseline drift on the PLC.

**Programme-mode events show no `src_user`** — the SIS vendor product may not capture programmer identity (some Triconex versions). Compensate by correlating to the engineering-workstation Windows account active at the time — a lookup against `wineventlog` logon events.

**OT-specific failure modes**:

- **SIS proof testing during outage**: a planned outage may include simultaneous proof tests of many valves. Ensure each gets its own MOC ticket and that the PST evidence is captured per valve.
- **SIL-3 valve duplex / TMR architectures**: a TMR SIS reports diagnostics from three logic solvers. Aggregate by SIF (Safety Instrumented Function) ID rather than per logic solver to avoid alert duplication.
- **Engineering workstation isolation gap**: an engineering workstation that loses connectivity to SIS may buffer commands and submit them in burst when reconnected. Detect by Cyber Vision flow analysis if seen on the SIS network.
- **Manufacturer firmware updates**: SIS firmware updates require functional-safety review per IEC 61511 §12. They are out of scope for routine patch UC-22.51.8; treat as a special path with dedicated MOC and FS review.
- **Operator HMI bypass screens**: some HMIs expose a 'bypass' control with operator-only permissions. Cross-correlate HMI screen actions with SIS bypass state for evidence.

## SPL

```spl
index=sis OR index=cybervision earliest=-2h@h
  (sourcetype IN ("schneider:triconex:diag","honeywell:safety_manager:diag","siemens:simatic_safety:diag","abb:safetyguard:diag","emerson:deltav_sis:diag","sis:bypass:alarm","cisco:cybervision:sis_event"))
| eval sis_asset_key=coalesce(sis_asset_key, asset_key, host)
| lookup sis_inventory.csv sis_asset_key OUTPUT asset_class sis_function tag_protection_level
| where isnotnull(asset_class)
| eval observed_at=_time
| eval event_class=coalesce(event_class, event_type)
| eval programme_mode_active = case(
    action IN ("programme_mode_enter","key_position=PROGRAM","keyswitch_program"), "true",
    action IN ("programme_mode_exit","key_position=RUN","keyswitch_run"), "false",
    1=1, null())
| eval bypass_switch_state=coalesce(bypass_state, bypass_switch_state)
| eval partial_stroke_test_status=coalesce(pst_status, partial_stroke_status)
| eval src_user=coalesce(src_user, user, operator)
| eval src_ip=coalesce(src_ip, src)
| join sis_asset_key event_class observed_at type=left [search index=moc sourcetype="servicenow:moc:ticket" state IN ("approved","implement") earliest=-30d | dedup moc_ticket_id | rename window_start AS moc_window_start window_end AS moc_window_end safety_review_ref AS safety_review_url | table sis_asset_key event_class moc_ticket_id moc_window_start moc_window_end safety_review_url]
| lookup sis_authorised_programmers.csv src_user OUTPUT programmer_authorised_flag
| eval programmer_authorised_flag=coalesce(programmer_authorised_flag, "false")
| eval unauthorised_programme_mode_flag = case(
    programme_mode_active="true" AND isnull(moc_ticket_id), "true",
    programme_mode_active="true" AND programmer_authorised_flag="false", "true",
    1=1, "false")
| eval unauthorised_bypass_flag = case(
    bypass_switch_state IN ("bypassed","override") AND isnull(moc_ticket_id), "true",
    1=1, "false")
| join sis_asset_key type=left [| inputlookup pst_schedule.csv | eval days_since_last_pst = round((now() - strptime(last_pst_at, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"))/86400) | eval pst_overdue = if(days_since_last_pst > pst_interval_days, "true", "false") | table sis_asset_key last_pst_at pst_interval_days days_since_last_pst pst_overdue]
| eval missing_pst_evidence_flag = if(pst_overdue="true", "true", "false")
| where unauthorised_programme_mode_flag="true" OR unauthorised_bypass_flag="true" OR missing_pst_evidence_flag="true" OR (event_class="trip_diagnostic" AND action="unexpected_trip")
| table observed_at sis_asset_key asset_class sis_function tag_protection_level src_user src_ip event_class action programme_mode_active bypass_switch_state partial_stroke_test_status moc_ticket_id safety_review_url unauthorised_programme_mode_flag unauthorised_bypass_flag missing_pst_evidence_flag
| sort - observed_at
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Single-value 'SIS programme-mode entries now', 'Bypass-switches engaged', 'PSTs overdue'. (Panel 2) Timeline of programme-mode entry/exit per SIS asset for the last 30 days. (Panel 3) Sortable table of every flagged event. (Panel 4) 13-month trend of programme-mode entries (informs maintenance-cadence reviews). (Panel 5) Valve-PST coverage matrix.

## Known False Positives

1. **Operator-station periodic diagnostic reads of SIS** — read-only diagnostic polling is not a programme-mode change. Suppression: only `action=programme_mode_enter` triggers the flag; `action=read_diagnostic` is informational.

2. **Vendor-vendor diagnostic during contracted service** — a vendor service engineer performing periodic certified service may legitimately enter programme mode with vendor authorisation. Suppression: time-bound entries in `sis_authorised_programmers.csv` referenced by vendor service contract.

3. **Partial-stroke test that completed but was not yet ingested** — PST completion event may lag by hours. Suppression: extend the PST grace by 48h or use the proof-test scheduling tool's API for real-time status.

4. **Maintenance-window bypass with permit-to-work** — some SIS valves may need permit-to-work bypass for mechanical work. Suppression: integrate the permit-to-work system into MOC; if no MOC, this is itself a finding under OTCC-2-3-2-1 (UC-22.51.9).

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `otcc_22_51_19_exceptions` keyed on `(sis_asset_key, event_class)` with `expires_at`, `reason`, `approved_by` (must be a functional-safety-certified manager).

## References

- [NCA OTCC — control list portal](https://nca.gov.sa/en/regulatory-documents/controls-list/3)
- [IEC 61511 — Functional safety — Safety instrumented systems for the process industry sector](https://webstore.iec.ch/publication/24241)
- [ISA TR84.00.09 — Cybersecurity related to the functional safety lifecycle](https://www.isa.org/products/tr84-00-09-2017-cybersecurity-related-to-the-functio)
